claim: WHO estimates 1.3 billion people have significant disability, 80 percent in LMICs, with prevalence rising due to population aging and NCDs, and documents systematic barriers to health and education access.

source.title: WHO Global Report on Health Equity for Persons with Disabilities

method: Reviewed WHO report chapters on prevalence, access barriers, and policy recommendations.
limitations: Disability prevalence estimates vary by measurement instrument.
Access-barrier data is qualitative and not systematically measured.

claim: A 2022 systematic review found disabled children in LMICs are 30-50 percent less likely to attend school, and disabled adults face 2-3 times higher barriers to healthcare access compared to non-disabled populations.

source.title: Disability and education access in low- and middle-income countries: a systematic review

method: Reviewed systematic search, meta-analysis of education gaps, and healthcare-access barriers.
limitations: Studies concentrated in a small number of LMICs.
